phases.title: Foundation
phases.blurb: The closed algebra over one type — the skill dir.
items: honeyc — the compiler (+ dietc domain proof)
ACCC / CORCC / SCCC — the three layers

phases.title: Project Surface
phases.blurb: Make it visible, publishable, and self-updating.

phases.title: Exchange
phases.blurb: Many skill trees in one repo, under a master.
items: A master system over the trees

phases.title: Self-Hosting & Self-Interpreter
phases.blurb: The literal self-interpreter — an SI for the chain dialect.
items: Self-interpreter (a Python dialect; defers to native ops)
SI MCP that is ALSO a skill

phases.title: Marketplace
phases.blurb: Programmatic publish; opt-in public; notify + grow.

phases.title: Federation
phases.blurb: Each emitted MCP becomes a repo; marketplaces federate. Plan: FEDERATION.md.
items: Contribution gate: validate + queue + gated promote ✓
Child marketplaces federate under the parent ✓ (reputation: aspirational)

phases.title: Skill OS
phases.blurb: Organize → observe → improve the global skill namespace. Shared human/agent interface.
items: Frontend: tree + analytics + problem-marking, launched from the root skill
Improve loop: reports → agent → skill-improver / create

phases.title: Plugin
phases.blurb: Bundle the whole thing as ONE installable Claude Code plugin.
items: bundles the capability skills + the SI MCP (done)

phases.title: GlyphSteer (research)
phases.blurb: Dual-regime retrieval steering: an LLM annotates a corpus with a compiled glyph vocabulary that steers search (lexical facet + dense direction) and is hidden at return time. HoneyC pointed at the index.
items: The sidecar split (indexed-form ≠ returned-form) + HIDE invariant ✓
One axis, two renderings (emoji=dense, ASCII tag=lexical) — FTS5 drops emoji ✓
Lexical regime: FTS5 facet, MRR 0.417→1.000 on the toy corpus ✓
Dense sidecar: ONNX/fastembed Docker image, no torch, /embed over HTTP ✓
Dense Q1-Q3 measured: emoji-direction real but model-dependent (bge-en collapses, XLM-R doesn't) ✓
Dense Q4 on a real corpus + arXiv short paper (aspirational)
